page: Google Cloud Platform c3-highcpu-44 is a compute-optimized virtual machine instance powered by an Intel Xeon 8481C processor. It features 44 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 22 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled, running at a base clock speed of 2.7 GHz. The instance is equipped with 88.0 GB of RAM, yielding a resource density of 2.0 GB of memory per core. It lacks local storage and GPU accelerators, relying entirely on network-attached storage.

The underlying x86_64 architecture supports advanced instruction sets, including Intel Advanced Vector Extensions (AVX-512) and Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) for deep learning and matrix multiplication. In benchmark evaluations, the instance demonstrates average performance across most standard workloads, with a PassMark CPU score of 47,734.78 and a Geekbench multi-core score of 15,396.0. It shows strong performance in specific areas, such as the PassMark CPU encryption test at 38,640.17 and Geekbench image processing at 16,046.0.

Memory bandwidth is measured at 114.34 GB/sec for large-block uncached reads and 47.67 GB/sec for writes. The hardware's AMX capabilities assist in small to medium LLM inference, achieving a strong prompt processing speed of 185.65 tokens/sec on a 7B model. However, a significant tradeoff is visible in large-scale models; text generation on a 70B model drops to a poor rate of 2.4 tokens/sec due to the lack of dedicated GPU acceleration and the 88.0 GB memory ceiling.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the c3-highcpu-44 offers a high density of dedicated compute resources relative to memory, making it a cost-effective choice for CPU-bound tasks that do not require massive memory footprints or local SSDs. It is appropriate for workloads such as software compilation, image processing, cryptography, medium-scale web serving, and CPU-based machine learning inference.
description: Google Cloud Platform c3-highcpu-44 is an x86_64 compute-optimized server featuring 44 dedicated vCPUs (22 physical cores) powered by an Intel Xeon 8481C processor at 2.7 GHz, paired with 88.0 GB of RAM. This instance lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. It supports advanced instruction sets like AVX-512 and Intel AMX, which drive strong performance in image processing and cryptographic operations. Benchmark results show average multi-core capabilities, with a Geekbench multi-core score of 15,396.0 and a PassMark CPU score of 47,734.78. While memory bandwidth is stable at 114.34 GB/sec for large uncached reads, the 2.0 GB per core memory ratio limits its effectiveness for memory-intensive databases or massive 70B LLM generation. The server is qualitatively cost-efficient for compute-heavy, CPU-bound workloads such as software compilation, cryptography, and medium-scale web serving.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform c3-highcpu-44 is a compute-optimized instance with 44 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 88 GB of RAM, and no local storage, designed for CPU-bound workloads and cryptographic tasks.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform c3-highcpu-44 is an x86_64 compute-optimized server featuring 44 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 88 GB of RAM, and no local storage.
tagline: A dedicated compute-optimized instance featuring advanced matrix extensions for high-throughput CPU-bound processing and cryptographic workloads.
bullet_points: 44 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs and 88.0 GB of RAM
Intel AMX and AVX-512 extensions for accelerated matrix math
Strong performance in cryptographic and image processing benchmarks
No local storage or GPU accelerators included
Designed for software compilation, cryptography, and CPU-bound web serving
